title: civikitchen profile
description: Canonical shape of images/profiles/<name>/profile.json — the declarative description of a civikitchen scenario (extensions, authx, API users). This is the single source of truth for the format; any tool that reads or writes these profiles validates against this schema.

description.description: One-line human summary of the scenario.

authx.description: AuthX configuration for headless API access.

header_cred.description: Accepted credential flows in the Authorization header.

apiUsers.description: API users created after install; credentials land in the boot log / credentials file.

permissions.description: CiviCRM permission strings granted to the user's role.

dependencies.description: Extensions the scenario needs. Applied in array order (deps before dependents).

dependency.description: One extension. Source is git (`repo` + `version`), the civicrm.org registry (`registry: true`), or core-bundled (neither — enable only).

repo.description: Git URL — cloned at `version` into the site's ext dir.

registry.description: true = fetch a packaged release from the civicrm.org registry (cv ext:download).

version.description: Git ref (tag/branch/commit) to check out. Only meaningful with `repo`.

track.description: Update policy for a git dependency. "pin": stay on the exact `version` (a tag or commit). "branch": `version` is a moving branch — follow its HEAD on update. Absent: pin when `version` is set, else follow the latest release tag. Requires `version`.

enable.description: Enable the extension after install (cv ext:enable). Absent/false = fetch only.

skip.description: Leave this extension unmanaged: never auto-install, update, or remove it. For locally-patched or manually-maintained extensions.

skipUf.description: Host CMS frameworks (CIVICRM_UF values) on which this extension is neither fetched nor enabled.

skipUfReason.description: Human-readable reason for skipUf, shown as a SKIP line in the boot log.

registry.description: Registry-sourced: no git repo, no git version/track — the release picks its own version.
